data.narrative:
ParityQC is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Wolfgang Lechner and Magdalena Hauser. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 identified comparable companies: 3 public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti, D-Wave, and Infleqtion.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The quantum computing comp universe lacks sufficient depth at the Series A stage—zero same-stage peers with adequate data density, one adjacent-stage comparable, and zero similarly-tiered private companies meeting filtering criteria. While 7 same-sector peers exist broadly, applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity in quantum technology approaches, maturity, and commercial traction across the peer set.

Without triangulated quantitative analysis from applicable methods, a defensible valuation range cannot be established from the available data. Proceeding would require either: (i) expansion to less-filtered comparables with explicit acknowledgment of their distance from ParityQC's stage and tier; (ii) alternative approaches such as venture capital method benchmarking against staged funding patterns in quantum computing; or (iii) collection of additional datapoints on late-stage or post-Series A quantum company transactions. Key uncertainties include ParityQC's revenue status, IP differentiation relative to the peer set, and capital efficiency to date—data not present in the current submission.
